openmeter/productcatalog/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter for the feature entity: implements feature.FeatureRepo (CRUD + archive + list) against openmeter/ent/db and exposes the Tx/WithTx/Self triad for ctx-transaction propagation. Primary constraint: every query returning a Feature must eager-load the Meter edge for MeterSlug backward compat.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method
patterns.description: Wrap each adapter method body with entutils.TransactingRepo so it rebinds to the ctx-bound Ent transaction instead of the raw client.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *featureDBAdapter) (feature.Feature, error) { ... })

patterns.name: WithMeter edge on every Feature query
patterns.description: All queries returning Feature rows must call .WithMeter(func(mq *db.MeterQuery){ mq.Select(dbmeter.FieldID, dbmeter.FieldKey) }) — omitting breaks MeterSlug for v1 callers.

patterns.name: UnitCost type-switch clears opposite columns
patterns.description: When writing UnitCost, clear the other type's DB columns so type switches are clean; MapFeatureEntity applies the same switch on read.

patterns.name: Dual MeterGroupByFilters column write
patterns.description: On write populate both AdvancedMeterGroupByFilters (typed) and MeterGroupByFilters (legacy map[string]string); on read prefer the advanced column if non-empty.

patterns.name: ArchiveFeature cross-checks active references
patterns.description: Before setting ArchivedAt, query active plans and active subscriptions referencing the feature; return ForbiddenError if any exist.

patterns.name: TxCreator + TxUser triad in transaction.go
patterns.description: transaction.go implements Tx() via HijackTx + NewTxDriver, WithTx() via db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig + NewPostgresFeatureRepo, Self() returning self — pure plumbing only.
patterns.example: func (e *featureDBAdapter) WithTx(ctx, tx *entutils.TxDriver) feature.FeatureRepo { txClient := db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return NewPostgresFeatureRepo(txClient.Client(), e.logger) }

key_file_guides.role: All feature CRUD: CreateFeature, UpdateFeature, GetByIdOrKey, ArchiveFeature, HasActiveFeatureForMeter, ListFeatures, MapFeatureEntity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing WithMeter edge returns broken MeterSlug; failing to clear the opposite UnitCost type's columns on update corrupts stored cost. FIXME: prefer models.NewGenericNotFoundError over feature.FeatureNotFoundError in new code.

key_file_guides.role: Implements entutils.TxCreator and entutils.TxUser[FeatureRepo] via HijackTx and NewTxClientFromRawConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add business logic here; must remain pure transaction plumbing.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests using testutils.InitPostgresDB + dbClient.Schema.Create; run in parallel behind a sync.Mutex.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests must create a real meter row first to satisfy the Feature.MeterID FK; skipping it fails the save.

anti_patterns: Using c.db directly in a helper accepting *db.Client without entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses the ctx transaction.
Omitting WithMeter on a new Feature query — breaks MeterSlug for v1 callers.
Adding validation or event publishing inside the adapter — belongs in featureConnector.
Returning feature.FeatureNotFoundError in new code — use models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
Editing files under openmeter/ent/db/ — generated; regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: Dual MeterGroupByFilters columns (legacy map + advanced typed filters)
decisions.rationale: The v1 API predates typed filter support; both columns are maintained for backward compat while new code uses the advanced column.

decisions.decision: TxCreator/TxUser split into a separate transaction.go
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate via ctx; Tx/WithTx/Self lets callers use entutils.TransactingRepo without leaking transaction types into domain interfaces.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new Feature field: write on create, clear/set on update, read in MapFeatureEntity

openmeter/productcatalog/driver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler package for the feature sub-domain: implements FeatureHandler (GetFeature, CreateFeature, ListFeatures, DeleteFeature) via the httptransport pattern with response mapping and centralised error encoding. Primary constraint: MeterSlug→MeterID resolution happens in the HTTP decoder, never in the connector.

patterns.name: httptransport.Handler per operation with typed aliases
patterns.description: Each operation returns a typed alias (e.g. GetFeatureHandler = httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...]); decoder, op func, encoder injected at construction. Never implement ServeHTTP directly.

patterns.name: Namespace resolution before any connector call
patterns.description: Every handler calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) (delegates to namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder); failure returns 500.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return models.NamespacedID{}, err }

patterns.name: Centralised error encoder via getErrorEncoder()
patterns.description: errors.go chains commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches per domain error type; always pass httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(getErrorEncoder()).

patterns.name: MeterSlug→MeterID resolution in decoder
patterns.description: In CreateFeature decoder, if parsedBody.MeterSlug != nil, call meterService.GetMeterByIDOrSlug before MapFeatureCreateInputsRequest; the connector takes MeterID only.
patterns.example: m, err := h.meterService.GetMeterByIDOrSlug(ctx, meter.GetMeterInput{Namespace: ns, IDOrSlug: *parsedBody.MeterSlug}); meterID = &m.ID

patterns.name: LLM pricing post-enrichment on GetFeature
patterns.description: After GetFeature, if UnitCost.Type == UnitCostTypeLLM and llmcostService != nil, call resolveLLMPricing then enrichFeatureResponseWithPricing.
patterns.example: if feat.UnitCost != nil && feat.UnitCost.Type == feature.UnitCostTypeLLM && h.llmcostService != nil { pricing := resolveLLMPricing(ctx, h.llmcostService, feat); enrichFeatureResponseWithPricing(&resp, pricing) }

patterns.name: Union response for paginated vs flat list
patterns.description: ListFeatures returns commonhttp.Union[[]api.Feature, pagination.Result[api.Feature]] — Option1 for flat list when page.IsZero(), Option2 for paginated.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureHandler interface and featureHandlers struct with GetFeature/CreateFeature/ListFeatures/DeleteFeature.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterSlug→MeterID must be resolved in the decoder; LLM pricing enrichment only fires when llmcostService is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: getErrorEncoder chains feature and meter domain errors to HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New domain error types must be registered here; unregistered errors fall through to 500.

key_file_guides.role: MapFeatureToResponse, MapFeatureCreateInputsRequest, domainUnitCostToAPI, apiUnitCostToDomain, resolveLLMPricing, enrichFeatureResponseWithPricing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: apiUnitCostToDomain dispatches via u.Discriminator(); a new UnitCostType requires a case here and in the domain type-switch.

anti_patterns: Calling connector methods before namespace resolution.
Returning namespace errors with a status other than 500.
Implementing ServeHTTP directly instead of httptransport.NewHandler/NewHandlerWithArgs.
Omitting httptransport.WithOperationName — breaks OTel span naming.
Hardcoding page-size limits instead of reusing commonhttp constants.

decisions.decision: MeterSlug→MeterID translation in the HTTP decoder, not the connector
decisions.rationale: The v1 API accepts meterSlug for backward compat while the domain works with MeterID; translating at the boundary keeps the connector independent of API versioning.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new v1 feature operation with LLM pricing enrichment

openmeter/productcatalog/feature.purpose: Domain package for feature entities: defines FeatureConnector (service with validation + event publishing), FeatureRepo (repository interface), Feature struct, UnitCost tagged types, MeterGroupByFilters, Watermill events, and feature-meter resolution. Primary constraint: all business logic and validation lives in featureConnector, never in the adapter.

patterns.name: FeatureConnector over FeatureRepo
patterns.description: External callers always go through FeatureConnector (meter validation, key uniqueness, event publishing); never call FeatureRepo directly from outside this package.

patterns.name: Input validation before repo delegation
patterns.description: All connector methods validate inputs (meter aggregation, ULID key guard, UnitCost.Validate/ValidateWithMeter) before delegating to featureRepo.
patterns.example: if _, err := ulid.Parse(feature.Key); err == nil { return Feature{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("Feature key cannot be a valid ULID")) }

patterns.name: Event publishing after successful repo mutation
patterns.description: Create/Update/ArchiveFeature each publish a typed Watermill event (implementing EventName/EventMetadata) after the repo write succeeds.
patterns.example: ev := NewFeatureCreateEvent(ctx, &createdFeature); if err := c.publisher.Publish(ctx, ev); err != nil { return createdFeature, fmt.Errorf("failed to publish: %w", err) }

patterns.name: nullable.Nullable[UnitCost] for partial updates
patterns.description: UpdateFeatureInputs.UnitCost distinguishes unset (validation error), null (clear), and value (update) via IsSpecified/IsNull/Get.
patterns.example: if input.UnitCost.IsNull() { /* clear */ } else if v, _ := input.UnitCost.Get(); v != nil { /* update */ }

patterns.name: FeatureMeterCollection dual-index resolution
patterns.description: ResolveFeatureMeters returns ByKey (latest non-archived per key) and ByID (all requested IDs); key lookup resolves to the latest active feature.

patterns.name: UnitCost mutual-exclusion validation
patterns.description: UnitCost.Validate() enforces that for LLM type each dimension has exactly one of property OR static value; new dimensions require updating both Validate and ValidateWithMeter.
patterns.example: if u.LLM.ProviderProperty != "" && u.LLM.Provider != "" { errs = append(errs, errors.New("provider and providerProperty are mutually exclusive")) }

key_file_guides.role: featureConnector implements FeatureConnector with all domain validation + event publishing; defines CreateFeatureInputs, UpdateFeatureInputs, ListFeaturesParams.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TODO: refactor to Service pattern — avoid adding more methods without considering that direction.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureRepo interface (CRUD + entutils.TxCreator + entutils.TxUser[FeatureRepo]) and ArchiveFeatureInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FeatureRepo embeds TxCreator/TxUser — any new adapter must implement Tx(), WithTx(), Self().

key_file_guides.role: UnitCost, ManualUnitCost, LLMUnitCost; Validate()/ValidateWithMeter(); LLMTokenType constants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New token types must go in the validTypes map inside Validate(); new dimensions need both Validate and ValidateWithMeter.
getLastFeatures prefers non-archived but returns the most recently archived if all are archived — callers must handle archived resolution.

key_file_guides.role: Watermill create/update/archive event structs implementing EventName() and EventMetadata().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Event version is hardcoded 'v1' — bump a new constant if payload changes to avoid breaking consumers.

key_file_guides.role: Feature struct, domain errors (FeatureNotFoundError, FeatureInvalidFiltersError, FeatureWithNameAlreadyExistsError, ForbiddenError), MeterGroupByFilters + conversions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FIXME: prefer models.NewGenericNotFoundError over FeatureNotFoundError in new code.

anti_patterns: Calling featureRepo directly from outside the package — go through FeatureConnector.
Publishing events before the repo write succeeds.
Setting UnitCost without Validate() and ValidateWithMeter() for LLM type.
Using context.Background() instead of the passed ctx in connector methods.
Adding a new UnitCostType without updating the validTypes map and the adapter/driver cases.

decisions.decision: FeatureConnector wraps FeatureRepo to keep validation and events in the domain layer
decisions.rationale: Adapters are pure persistence; validation and Watermill side-effects belong in the connector so they run regardless of adapter.

decisions.decision: Features support key-based versioning via archive (one active + many archived per key)
decisions.rationale: Plans/subscriptions reference features by key; archiving without replacement lets existing references resolve to the most recent version via getLastFeatures.

openmeter/productcatalog/featureresolver.purpose: Adapts feature.FeatureConnector into the productcatalog.FeatureResolver/NamespacedFeatureResolver interface and resolves rate-card feature references (by ID and/or key) to concrete features, attaching field-prefixed validation errors. Breaks the import cycle between productcatalog and the feature package.

patterns.name: Resolver wraps FeatureConnector
patterns.description: New(service feature.FeatureConnector) returns a resolver; WithNamespace returns a namespacedResolver. Both satisfy productcatalog.FeatureResolver via var _ assertions.

patterns.name: BatchResolve via CollectAll over ListFeatures
patterns.description: BatchResolve fans the id/key list into one paginated feature.ListFeaturesParams query (pagination.CollectAll + NewPaginator), building a map keyed by both ID and key.
patterns.example: features, err := pagination.CollectAll(ctx, pagination.NewPaginator(func(ctx, page) (pagination.Result[feature.Feature], error) { return r.service.ListFeatures(ctx, feature.ListFeaturesParams{...}) }))

patterns.name: ID/key conflict detection
patterns.description: Resolve checks f.ID == *id and f.Key == *key after lookup, returning GenericConflictError on mismatch and GenericNotFoundError on absence.

patterns.name: Field-prefixed aggregated rate-card errors
patterns.description: ResolveFeaturesForRateCards accumulates per-ratecard errors wrapped with models.ErrorWithFieldPrefix(NewFieldSelectorGroup(...)) and joins them via NewNillableGenericValidationError.

patterns.name: SetFeature back onto the rate card
patterns.description: On successful resolution each rate card is mutated in place via rc.SetFeature(&f.ID, &f.Key) so downstream code sees both ID and key populated.

key_file_guides.role: New, resolver, namespacedResolver; Resolve, BatchResolve, WithNamespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BatchResolve returns (nil, nil) for an empty input list; Resolve requires at least one of id/key non-empty.

key_file_guides.role: ResolveFeaturesForRateCards — bulk-resolves features for a RateCards collection and reports field-scoped validation errors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns nil for nil/empty rate cards; mutates rate cards in place via SetFeature; uses productcatalog.ErrRateCardFeatureNotFound / ErrRateCardFeatureMismatch sentinels.

anti_patterns: Calling feature.FeatureConnector directly from productcatalog plan/addon code instead of through this resolver — reintroduces the import direction this package exists to break.
Returning a bare error from ResolveFeaturesForRateCards instead of the joined NewNillableGenericValidationError — loses field-path context.
Resolving with neither id nor key set — Resolve returns an error.

decisions.decision: Resolver lives in featureresolver, not feature (NOTE comment: should move under feature after refactor)
decisions.rationale: productcatalog needs to resolve features without importing the feature package's connector directly; a thin adapter keeps the dependency direction clean.

openmeter/productcatalog/http.purpose: Shared HTTP utilities for the productcatalog domain: ValidationErrorEncoder (wraps ValidationIssues into problem+json), bidirectional API↔domain mapping for RateCards/Prices/EntitlementTemplates/Discounts, and ResourceKind constants. Consumed by plan, addon, and subscription HTTP handlers to avoid duplication.

patterns.name: ValidationErrorEncoder with ResourceKind
patterns.description: ValidationErrorEncoder(kind ResourceKind) converts []models.ValidationIssue into a validationErrors extension on the problem+json 400 response; attach per-resource-kind in handler options.

patterns.name: Discriminator-based RateCard mapping
patterns.description: AsRateCard dispatches on r.Discriminator(); FromRateCard switches on r.Type(). New RateCard types require cases in both directions.
patterns.example: rType, _ := r.Discriminator(); switch rType { case string(productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCardType): return AsFlatFeeRateCard(r) }

patterns.name: Price type dispatch via price.Type()
patterns.description: FromRateCardUsageBasedPrice dispatches on price.Type() (Flat, Unit, Tiered, Dynamic, Package); new price models need a case in both FromRateCardUsageBasedPrice and AsRateCard.
patterns.example: switch price.Type() { case productcatalog.FlatPriceType: ...; case productcatalog.TieredPriceType: ... }

patterns.name: UsageBasedRateCard requires non-nil BillingCadence
patterns.description: In FromRateCard for UsageBasedRateCardType, billingCadence != nil is mandatory (return error if nil); FlatFeeRateCard BillingCadence is optional.
patterns.example: if billingCadence == nil { return resp, errors.New("invalid UsageBasedRateCard: billing cadence must be set") }

key_file_guides.role: FromRateCard, AsRateCard, AsFlatFeeRateCard, AsUsageBasedRateCard, FromRateCardUsageBasedPrice, FromTaxConfig, FromEntitlementTemplate, FromDiscounts and inverses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UsageBasedRateCard requires non-nil BillingCadence; all functions must stay pure conversions with no side-effects.

key_file_guides.role: ValidationErrorEncoder and private validationError type with AsErrorExtension serialization.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns false (not handled) if models.AsValidationIssues yields no issues — falls through to the generic encoder.

key_file_guides.role: ResourceKind string type with ResourceKindPlan and ResourceKindAddon constants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new ResourceKind constants here when introducing new top-level productcatalog resources.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or side-effects to mapping functions — they must be pure bidirectional conversions.
Skipping the error return from FromRateCard/AsRateCard — both can fail on unsupported types.
Importing this package from outside productcatalog without knowing it imports api (v1) types directly.

decisions.decision: Separate http package for shared productcatalog HTTP utilities
decisions.rationale: RateCard and price mappings are reused across plan, addon, and subscription handlers; centralising avoids duplication and keeps conversion logic in one reviewable place.
